message:
feat(core): integrate MVCC GC scheduler for incremental version-chain pruning

Adds three new fields to Connection — GcScheduler, GcTodo, and
gc_clock_millis — that together drive adaptive, incremental garbage
collection of the MVCC version store without requiring a stop-the-world
sweep.

GcScheduler derives its tick frequency from version-chain pressure metrics
(chain length, total retired versions) so GC runs more often under write-
heavy workloads and backs off during read-dominant periods. Each tick calls
compute_gc_horizon() to determine the oldest commit sequence still visible
to any active snapshot, then prunes version entries below that horizon via
the GcTodo per-process touched-page queue.

Additional small fixes included in this commit:
- Remove suppression of clippy::unnecessary_trailing_comma (no longer needed)
- Remove premature `break` in the Text/Blob branch of the value-scanning
  loop that was short-circuiting iteration when it should have continued to
  the next row via cursor.next()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
